refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained baselines/calibration updates. High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance requirements are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and operator identity; (c) history log captures all updates with reason codes and approval status; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline → implementation is complete and queryable.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirement set (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, operator, approval status).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain auditable history of all requirement and calibration updates with change reason and approval chain.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability query interface linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baseline → system implementation.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests mission context via API, executes requirement derivation algorithms (cycle time, energy, availability, safety margin calculations), and writes baseline + history to a managed database with full audit logging.

DP-B: Distributed Derivation – Performance requirement derivation logic embedded in Vehicle control software, with baseline/history managed by a separate cloud-based service; requires inter-module synchronization.
DP-C: Manual Baseline Entry with Post-Hoc Validation – Site Manager manually enters performance targets; system validates against mission context and logs changes; minimal automation.

DP-D: Hybrid Derivation with Real-Time Feedback Loop – Automatic derivation engine (DP-A) augmented with continuous real-time performance monitoring that triggers re-derivation and baseline updates when actual performance deviates beyond thresholds.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine with integrated baseline and history management. This DP concentrates requirement derivation logic, baseline recording, and audit trail in a single, testable, auditable module. It minimizes coupling between Vehicle control and performance management, supports high safety criticality via centralized validation and approval workflows, and enables traceability queries without cross-module synchronization.

potential_sources: DP-A requires input from worksite mission context (Site Manager, Teleoperation module) – managed via well-defined Tasking interface.
DP-A must communicate derived requirements and baseline status to Vehicle control and Safety Services – managed via Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces.
DP-A depends on a persistent baseline/history database – internal to the module, no external coupling.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is minimal and interface-mediated. No hidden dependencies on Vehicle internal state or Teleoperation real-time loops. Decoupling is strong because derivation is independent of vehicle execution; it informs execution but does not depend on it.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available in structured form (terrain type, payload mass, cycle count, safety constraints) from Site Manager or external planning system.
ASSUMPTION: Performance derivation algorithms (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margin calculations) are well-defined, validated, and stable.
RISK: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derived requirements may be invalid; mitigation: require explicit validation gate before baseline approval.
RISK: Derivation algorithms may not capture all safety-critical edge cases; mitigation: Safety Manager review and approval required before baseline activation.
RISK: High-frequency re-derivation (if triggered by real-time feedback) could create audit trail bloat; mitigation: implement change-threshold logic to avoid spurious updates.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the approval workflow: who (Site Manager, Safety Manager, System Engineer) must approve a derived baseline before it becomes active? Recommend: Site Manager proposes mission context → System derives requirements → Safety Manager reviews and approves → baseline locked until next formal update cycle. This ensures safety criticality is honored.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integration boundary. It contains or orchestrates all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and provides the unified interface for performance requirement derivation, baseline management, and history tracking. The Performance Derivation Engine (DP-A) is realized as a core service within NGHE Solution, responsible for ingesting mission context, executing derivation algorithms, recording baselines, and maintaining audit trails.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager or external planning system. Used to populate inputs to the Performance Derivation Engine.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmits derived performance requirements, current baseline status, and requirement change notifications to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules. Also receives actual performance data (cycle time, energy consumption, availability metrics) for comparison and re-derivation triggers.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraints and approval status from Safety Services. Transmits derived safety margins and requirement updates for Safety Manager review and approval before baseline activation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for baseline/history database synchronization, audit log transmission to external compliance systems, and remote access for Site Manager and Safety Manager to query and approve baselines.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration boundary and the natural home for cross-cutting concerns like performance requirement derivation, baseline management, and audit trails. The Performance Derivation Engine is a core service that must be visible and controllable at the system level. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle module is responsible for executing missions and reporting actual performance. It should NOT contain the derivation engine or baseline management logic. Placing DP-A in Vehicle would couple requirement derivation to vehicle control logic, violate separation of concerns, and make audit trails vehicle-specific rather than system-wide. High diffusion risk: requirement derivation is a system-level concern, not a vehicle-level concern.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operator interface and real-time control. Performance requirement derivation is not a teleoperation concern. Placing DP-A in Teleoperation would create tight coupling between requirement management and operator interface, making it difficult to support autonomous or mixed-autonomy modes. High diffusion risk.

Safety Services is responsible for safety monitoring, alerts, and enforcement. While Safety Services must review and approve derived safety margins, the derivation engine itself is not a safety service function. Placing DP-A in Safety Services would conflate requirement derivation with safety enforcement, making it difficult to separate concerns and audit trails. Moderate-to-high diffusion risk. Safety Services should be a consumer and approver of derived requirements, not the owner.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Performance Derivation Engine (DP-A) is best contained within NGHE Solution because: (1) it is a system-level service that must integrate mission context from Site Manager, derive requirements for Vehicle and Teleoperation, and coordinate approval with Safety Services; (2) baseline and history management are system-wide concerns that must be visible and auditable across all subsystems; (3) containment in NGHE Solution minimizes coupling to Vehicle or Teleoperation execution logic and preserves separation of concerns; (4) external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are already defined at the system level and provide clean integration points.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic 'god module' that owns too many concerns, it may become difficult to test, deploy, and maintain. MITIGATION: Ensure NGHE Solution is primarily an orchestration and integration layer; the Performance Derivation Engine should be a well-encapsulated service with clear API boundaries.

RISK: If baseline/history database is not properly isolated, it could become a bottleneck or single point of failure. MITIGATION: Implement database replication, backup, and recovery procedures; ensure audit logs are immutable and tamper-evident.

RISK: If approval workflow is not clearly defined, baselines could be activated without proper safety review. MITIGATION: Implement explicit approval state machine (Proposed → Reviewed → Approved → Active) with role-based access control and audit logging.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define the internal architecture of NGHE Solution to clarify how the Performance Derivation Engine is packaged and interfaced. Recommend: (1) Performance Derivation Engine as a microservice or well-defined software component with published API; (2) Baseline/History Database as a separate, managed data store with replication and backup; (3) Approval Workflow Engine as a separate service that enforces role-based approval gates; (4) Traceability Query Service as a separate API that links worksite inputs → derived requirements → baseline → implementation. This internal decomposition will make NGHE Solution more maintainable and testable.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR (CR-NGHE-005 Refined) clearly specifies what the system must do: derive, record, and manage performance requirements from worksite mission context with full traceability and audit trail. DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) directly addresses this by providing a dedicated, testable mechanism for derivation, baseline recording, and history management. The mapping is clear and non-ambiguous. No hidden assumptions or missing requirements.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK (Low): DP-A requires inputs from Tasking (mission context) and outputs to Telemetry (derived requirements) and SafetyAlert (safety margins). These are well-defined external interfaces; no diffusion into Vehicle or Teleoperation internals.
DIFFUSION RISK (Low): Baseline/history database is internal to NGHE Solution; no diffusion to other modules.
DIFFUSION RISK (Moderate): Approval workflow involves Safety Manager (external stakeholder); ensure approval state is clearly communicated via SafetyAlert interface to avoid implicit dependencies.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct containment boundary. It is the system-level integration point where mission context (from Site Manager), vehicle capabilities (from Vehicle module), and safety constraints (from Safety Services) converge. Containment score of 95 reflects strong alignment. No alternative module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) is a suitable home for DP-A without introducing high diffusion and coupling risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible and well-scoped. The refined FR specifies five sub-FRs (mission context ingestion, requirement computation, baseline recording, history management, traceability queries) that are independently testable. Each sub-FR can be verified via unit tests (derivation algorithms), integration tests (interface contracts), and system tests (end-to-end traceability). Acceptance criteria (documented performance targets, traceable to worksite inputs, maintained baselines/calibration updates) are directly verifiable against the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear. CR-NGHE-005 is allocated to NGHE Solution. Sub-FRs are allocated to internal services (Performance Derivation Engine, Baseline/History Database, Approval Workflow Engine, Traceability Query Service) that will be defined in the next decomposition level. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are clearly identified and owned by NGHE Solution.

recommended_revisions: REVISION 1: Clarify the approval workflow in the refined FR. Recommend adding a sub-FR: 'CR-NGHE-005.6: Enforce role-based approval gates (Site Manager proposes, Safety Manager approves, System Engineer validates) before baseline activation.' This ensures safety criticality is honored and audit trail is complete.

REVISION 2: Define performance derivation algorithms in a separate requirements document or design specification. The refined FR assumes algorithms exist; recommend explicit validation that algorithms are correct, complete, and safe before implementation.

REVISION 3: Specify baseline versioning and rollback procedures. Recommend adding a sub-FR: 'CR-NGHE-005.7: Support baseline versioning and rollback to previous approved baselines in case of derivation errors or mission context changes.' This ensures operational flexibility and safety.

REVISION 4: Define the scope of 'worksite mission context.' Recommend creating a data dictionary that specifies all mission context inputs (terrain type, payload mass, cycle count, safety constraints, environmental conditions, etc.) and their valid ranges. This will guide implementation and testing.

decision.justification: The refined FR (CR-NGHE-005 Refined) is well-scoped, non-ambiguous, and directly addresses the acceptance criteria. DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) is a sound design choice that minimizes coupling and supports high safety criticality. NGHE Solution is the correct containment boundary with a containment score of 95. All gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass. However, the refined FR is not atomic; it decomposes into five sub-FRs (mission context ingestion, requirement computation, baseline recording, history management, traceability queries) that require further decomposition. Recommend proceeding to the next level to decompose each sub-FR and define the internal architecture of NGHE Solution (Performance Derivation Engine, Baseline/History Database, Approval Workflow Engine, Traceability Query Service).
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input via Tasking interface.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirement set (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, operator, approval status) in persistent baseline database.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain auditable history of all requirement and calibration updates with change reason and approval chain.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability query interface linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baseline → system implementation.
CR-NGHE-005.6: Enforce role-based approval gates (Site Manager proposes, Safety Manager approves, System Engineer validates) before baseline activation.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available in structured form (terrain type, payload mass, cycle count, safety constraints) from Site Manager or external planning system.
Performance derivation algorithms (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margin calculations) are well-defined, validated, and stable.
Safety Manager has authority to approve derived safety margins before baseline activation.
Baseline/history database is persistent, replicated, and backed up to ensure data integrity and availability.
Traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline → implementation is queryable and auditable.
NGHE Solution is the system-level integration boundary and owns cross-cutting concerns like performance requirement derivation and baseline management.
open_risks: RISK: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derived requirements may be invalid. MITIGATION: Require explicit validation gate before baseline approval.
RISK: Derivation algorithms may not capture all safety-critical edge cases. MITIGATION: Safety Manager review and approval required before baseline activation.
RISK: High-frequency re-derivation could create audit trail bloat. MITIGATION: Implement change-threshold logic to avoid spurious updates.
RISK: Baseline/history database could become a bottleneck or single point of failure. MITIGATION: Implement database replication, backup, and recovery procedures.
RISK: Approval workflow could be bypassed or circumvented. MITIGATION: Implement role-based access control and immutable audit logging.
evidence_needed: Specification of worksite mission context data model (terrain types, payload ranges, cycle count ranges, safety constraints, environmental conditions).
Validation of performance derivation algorithms (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margin calculations) against historical data and expert judgment.
Definition of approval workflow state machine (Proposed → Reviewed → Approved → Active) with role-based access control.
Design of baseline/history database schema with replication, backup, and recovery procedures.
Design of traceability query API that links worksite inputs → derived requirements → baseline → implementation.
Test plan for CR-NGHE-005 and sub-FRs, including unit tests (derivation algorithms), integration tests (interface contracts), and system tests (end-to-end traceability).
Safety analysis (FMEA, FTA) for performance requirement derivation and baseline management to identify and mitigate safety-critical failure modes.
